accommodation-modal.update-integration-p:
連携期間を短縮すると、それ以降の期間の同期は停止します。その期間の管理はチャネル側で行ってください。
（チャネルが販売期間を制限している場合は、チャネルのポリシーが適用されます。）

連携期間を延長すると、一部のチャネルで未設定の商品が販売可能な状態で表示される場合があります。運用ポリシーに沿って設定してください。  
（チャネル側で販売期間が制限されている場合は、そのチャネルのポリシーが適用されます。）
連携期間の更新に失敗しました。
連携期間を更新しました。

昨日と本日を含む、30日から180日以内の連動期間を設定できます。  
設定した連動期間の在庫と料金がチャネルに同期されます。  
（チャネル側で販売期間が制限されている場合は、チャネルのポリシーが優先されます。）

· 10室までは室数課金ではなく基本料金が適用されます。

· キャンセルされた購読はいつでも再購読可能です。 
· 年額決済後7日以内の解約：決済額から「割引価格」での利用日分を除いて返金。 
· 年額決済後7日以降の解約：「通常価格」での利用日分を除いて返金。 
· 解約後は復元不可。再利用には再購読が必要です。

サブスクリプション案内および返金ポリシー

· サブスクリプション料金には消費税が含まれており、解約しても次回の請求日まではサービスを利用できます。
· 解約されたサブスクリプションは、いつでも再加入できます。
· 即時解約の場合、残りの利用期間分は元のお支払い方法に返金されます。
· 請求日から7日以降に解約を申請した場合、サブスクリプション料金の10%が違約金として差し引かれ、90%が返金されます。
· 解約されたサブスクリプションは再有効化できません。サービスを再開するには、新たにサブスクリプションを購入する必要があります。
· 定期支払いが失敗した場合や未払いが発生した場合、1日の猶予期間が付与されます。（猶予期間内に支払いが完了しないとサービスが停止されるため、ご注意ください。）
· 失敗したサブスクリプションの支払いが後で正常に処理された場合、その時点からサブスクリプションが再開されます。

terms.private:
Vendit Co., Ltd. (“the Company”) complies with the Personal Information Protection Act and other related laws to protect the personal information of representatives and businesses managing accommodations (“Customers”). This Privacy Policy applies to the Vendit Channel Management System (“VCMS” or “the Solution”), including its online, mobile, and application-based services, and outlines how the Company collects, uses, and safeguards personal information.

1. Purpose of Processing Personal Information and Items Collected
The Company processes personal information for the following purposes and collects the following items:

Purpose of Processing
• Providing, maintaining, and managing the Solution.
• Membership registration and management.

Personal Information Collected
• Mandatory Information:
• User ID, password
• Business registration number, business name, address
• Business type and category
• Contact person’s name, email, and phone number
• Digital certificate ID and password
• OTA registration details

2. Provision of Personal Information to Third Parties
The Company does not disclose personal information to third parties without prior consent unless:
• It aligns with the original purpose of collection or customary business practices.
• Necessary safety measures, such as pseudonymization or encryption, are in place.

3. Retention and Usage Period of Personal Information
The Company retains personal information for the following periods:
• Standard Retention: Up to five years after membership termination.
• Legal Requirements: Certain data may be retained longer, as mandated by law.
• Login records: 3 months
• Communication records: 12 months

4. Destruction of Personal Information
Personal information is destroyed promptly after the retention period expires or the purpose of processing is achieved.

Destruction Procedure
• Data is identified and approved for deletion by the Data Protection Officer.

Destruction Methods
• Printed data: Shredded or incinerated.
• Electronic data: Irrevocably deleted using secure technical methods.

5. Rights of Customers and How to Exercise Them
Customers have the right to:
• Access, correct, delete, or restrict processing of their personal information.
• Withdraw consent or terminate membership.
Requests can be submitted via email, phone, or written communication to the Data Protection Officer. The Company will promptly address these requests.

6. Use of Automated Information Collection Devices (Cookies)
The Company uses cookies to enhance user experience and optimize service delivery.

Purpose of Cookies
• Ensure secure and efficient service access.
• Provide personalized experiences.

Managing Cookies
• Users can adjust browser settings to refuse or delete cookies. However, this may limit certain functionalities.

7. Technical and Administrative Safeguards
To ensure the security of personal information, the Company implements:

Technical Measures
• Regular data backups and antivirus software.
• Encryption for secure data transmission.
• Firewalls and access control to prevent unauthorized access.

Administrative Measures
• Staff training on data protection policies.
• Limiting access to authorized personnel only.

8. Data Protection Officer
The Data Protection Officer oversees compliance with data protection laws and addresses customer inquiries.
• Name: Eungyu Lee
• Position: CTO, Development Team
• Email: eungyu@vendit.co.kr
• Phone: +82-1644-5442

9. Personal Information Inquiries and Complaints
For concerns related to the handling of personal information or potential breaches, Customers may contact the Data Protection Officer. The Company will cooperate with investigations and strive for prompt resolution.

10. Changes to the Privacy Policy
This Privacy Policy is effective as of June 1, 2023.
Any updates, additions, or modifications will be announced in advance via the “Notices” section of the Company’s website.
terms.service:
Article 1 (Purpose)
These Terms and Conditions define the rights, obligations, and responsibilities of Vendit Co., Ltd. (“the Company”) and users (“Users”) regarding the use of the Vendit Channel Management System (“the Solution”), which includes online, mobile, and application-based services.

Article 2 (Definitions)
1. Solution: A system provided by the Company that supports functions such as member management, room sales record management, and payment program integration.
2. Service: Room sales record management services provided through the Solution.
3. Room Sales Record Management: Automated login to online travel agencies (OTAs) using stored credentials to manage and review room sales records collectively.
4. Member: A person or entity registered with the Solution who continuously uses the services provided by the Company.
5. User: A Member accessing the Solution under these Terms and Conditions.
6. Service Fees: Fees charged for using the Solution’s basic and additional services.

Article 3 (Notice, Explanation, and Amendment of Terms)
1. The Company will disclose these Terms and Conditions, along with necessary business and contact information, on the Solution’s homepage for easy access.
2. The Company may amend these Terms in compliance with applicable laws.
3. Amendments will be announced at least seven (7) days prior to their effective date. For changes unfavorable to Users, a notice period of thirty (30) days will be provided.

Article 4 (Formation of the Agreement)
1. The Agreement is established when a User consents to these Terms, applies for the Solution, and the Company approves the application.
2. Users must provide accurate information during registration, and the Company may verify their identity.
3. The Company reserves the right to withhold or reject applications under specific conditions, such as false information or unauthorized use.

Article 5 (Provision of Services)
1. The Company provides services, including automated login to OTAs, room sales record management, and related functions.
2. Services may be modified due to legal, business, or technical changes, with Users promptly notified of significant modifications.

Article 6 (Service Interruption)
1. Services may be temporarily suspended for maintenance, technical issues, or emergencies.
2. Users will be compensated for damages caused by interruptions unless the Company proves the absence of negligence.

Article 7 (Membership Registration)
1. Membership is granted when Users register and agree to these Terms.
2. The Company may deny membership for reasons such as false registration or failure to provide required documents.

Article 8 (User Obligations)
1. Users must provide accurate information and comply with these Terms and applicable laws.
2. Users must secure their account credentials and report any unauthorized access immediately.

Article 9 (Service Fees)
1. Users are required to pay fees as determined by the Company.
2. Additional charges may apply for optional features.

Article 10 (Payment Methods)
1. Service fees are primarily paid via credit card.
2. Payments may be recurring monthly, one-time for additional features, or annual.

Article 11 (Exclusion of Consumer Protection Laws)
These Terms govern business-to-business transactions, and consumer protection laws do not apply.

Article 12 (Use and Termination of the Solution)
1. The Solution may be used immediately after the Agreement is established.
2. Users may terminate use at any time, but all outstanding fees must be settled.

Article 13 (Membership Withdrawal and Disqualification)
1. Members may withdraw at any time by notifying the Company.
2. The Company may limit, suspend, or revoke membership for violations of these Terms.

Article 14 (Notices to Users)
1. Notices will be sent to the User’s registered contact details.
2. General notices may be posted on the Solution’s homepage for at least one week.

Article 15 (Modification and Cancellation of Agreements)
1. Users may request modifications or cancellations of agreements, subject to applicable laws.
2. The Company is not liable for modifications unless proven to result from negligence.

Article 16 (Fulfillment of Agreements)
1. Users must fulfill their contractual obligations as specified in the Agreement.
2. The Company may request information about contract execution for advertising or promotional purposes.

Article 17 (Information Protection)
